rationale: Required-first allocation preserves original response order; ingredient reads are stably ordered; repeated reads match; uncertainty, semantic dates, non-mutation, mapping attribution, and recipe text are covered. Grocery/cooking integration may proceed, while rendered/final evidence remains outstanding.

worker_package.objective: Deliver one integrated grocery-to-cooking Pantry slice: definitive projected-demand shortages with uncertain lines kept non-numeric; persisted formula inputs and meal/recipe/product provenance; manual override preservation across regeneration; explicit location-aware actor-attributed idempotent purchased-item intake; explicit cook confirmation/adjustment before atomic FEFO deduction; recipe/meal/list/cook/ActorContext event linkage; safe conflict-aware undo; and linked leftover batches. Use additive migration 0020 and preserve all current dirty work.
verify: pnpm exec prettier --check <T015 source/tests/docs excluding SQL and actively shared docs/data-model.md>
pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/pantry-grocery-cooking.test.ts tests/unit/cooking.test.ts
stop_if: 0020 exists, applied migration must change, or journal cannot extend additively.
Uncertain/incompatible stock would become exact or availability formula files must change.
Regeneration would overwrite manual fields/additions or intake cannot be idempotent.
Deduction occurs before confirmation, bypasses FEFO without explicit adjustment, lacks atomic attribution, or has unsafe undo/leftover conflicts.

Dirty planning/list/cooking/schema/docs/nutrition/UI work cannot be preserved; files outside scope or weakened origin/ActorContext/credential boundaries are required; normal household DB would be mutated; or shared .next ownership is required.

missing_evidence: Implemented grocery/cooking migration, provenance, intake, confirmation, deduction, undo, leftovers, rendered UX, full build, and final oracle.
required_board_updates: Activate one T015 Worker; then require milestone Judge and rendered UX review before T999.
